  Pet food recall expanded after rickets, elevated vitamin levels reported:
  Return ASAP - MassLive

summary: >-
  Revival Animal Health expanded its recall of Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine
  Milk Replacer, Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacer, and Breeder's Edge
  Foster Care GM (goat milk) Milk Replacer after testing confirmed variable
  vitamin D levels. The recall was first issued April 17 and expanded June 25.
  Some products contain too much vitamin D, while others contain too little. Two
  rickets complaints have been reported. Puppies consuming low vitamin D may
  develop bone deformities, swollen joints, and lameness; elevated levels can
  cause vomiting, diarrhea, appetite loss, weakness, lethargy, reduced growth,
  weight loss, and at very high levels, kidney calcification. Affected products
  were sold nationwide online and in retail pet stores. Owners should stop using
  the products immediately and contact Revival Animal Health at 1-877-870-4059
  for a full refund.

location: 'nationwide, United States (online and retail pet stores)'
---
Revival Animal Health expanded its recall of Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine Milk Replacer, Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacer, and Breeder's Edge Foster Care GM (goat milk) Milk Replacer after testing confirmed variable vitamin D levels. The recall was first issued April 17 and expanded June 25. Some products contain too much vitamin D, while others contain too little. Two rickets complaints have been reported. Puppies consuming low vitamin D may develop bone deformities, swollen joints, and lameness; elevated levels can cause vomiting, diarrhea, appetite loss, weakness, lethargy, reduced growth, weight loss, and at very high levels, kidney calcification. Affected products were sold nationwide online and in retail pet stores. Owners should stop using the products immediately and contact Revival Animal Health at 1-877-870-4059 for a full refund.

[Read the full report on Google News →](https://news.google.?oc=5)
